lines.en_lit: where one took out a donkey with it.

“And he shall slaughter it,” that no other should be slaughtered with it. “Before him”; according to Rav, that he should not divert his attention from it. According to Shmuel, that a non-priest can slaughter and Elazar observes.

“And Elazar the priest shall take some of its blood with his finger” According to Shmuel, to return it to Elazar, According to Rav, this is a restrictive expression following a restrictive expression, And a restrictive expression following a restrictive expression only to amplify that even a common

“And the priest shall take cedar wood, and hyssop, and a strip of crimson” According to Shmuel, even a common priest According to Rav, is necessary enter your mind to say since do not the heifer itself, they do not require a priest teaches us
“Then the priest shall wash his clothes” in his priestly “And the priest shall be impure until evening” in generations in his priestly

well according to the one who said for generations by a common priest. But according to the one who said for generations by a High Priest, now that we require the High Priest, is necessary in his priestly Yes, a matter that be derived by an a fortiori inference, the verse unnecessarily writes it

“And a man who is pure shall gather up the ashes of the heifer and place them” “a man” to qualify a non-priest “pure” to qualify a woman “and place” one who has intelligence to to place excluding a deaf-mute, an imbecile, and a minor, they do not have intelligence to to place
We learned there, Everyone qualified to sanctify except for a deaf-mute, an imbecile, and a minor. Rabbi Yehuda qualifies a minor, but disqualifies a woman and a hermaphrodite.

What is the reason of the Rabbis, As it is written: “And they shall take for the impure of the ashes of the burning of the purification from sin, Those whom I disqualified for you for gathering I have disqualified for you for sanctification; and those whom I have qualified for you for gathering I have qualified for you sanctification.
And Rabbi Yehuda If so, let the verse say: And he shall take. What “and they shall take”? that even a minor, who I disqualified there here, he is qualified.

From where a woman “and he shall put” and not: And she shall put. the Rabbis if the Merciful One had written: And he shall take…and he shall put, I would have said unless one takes and one puts the Merciful One writes “and they shall take”

And if the Merciful One had written And they shall take…and they shall put, I would have said unless two take and two put. the Merciful One writes “and they shall take” “and he shall put” that even two take and one puts

“And a man who is pure shall take hyssop and dip it into the water” According to the Rabbis, “man” but not a woman, “pure” to qualify a minor And according to Rabbi Yehuda, man but not a minor, pure to qualify a woman

raises an objection Everyone is qualified to sprinkle a person whose sexual organs are concealed [tumtum], and a hermaphrodite [androginus], and a woman. And a minor who has intelligence, a woman assist him and he sprinkles
